commitMessage:
Handles the TypePattern DOM API modifications in the JDT.UI

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1994
categoryReason: This change updates internal JDT API usage to support new Java language features.
summary: This commit modernizes internal JDT UI code for Pattern Matching in `instanceof` expressions. It replaces a direct call to `TypePattern.getPatternVariable()` with a ternary expression that conditionally calls either `getPatternVariable()` or `getPatternVariable2()` based on the AST API level (JLS22 or higher). This adapts to API changes introduced for Java 22, ensuring backward compatibility within the JDT's internal AST processing. The transformation is a direct expression replacement, fully supported by the DSL, and guarded by `sourceVersionGE(22)`.

commitMessage:
Fix NPE when opening quick type hierarchy twice #2002

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2002
categoryReason: This commit fixes an internal bug within JDT's UI components.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support deleting entire methods, moving blocks of statements between methods, adding new local variable declarations, and wrapping existing code blocks in new conditional (if) statements. These capabilities are not currently available in the TriggerPattern DSL.
summary: This commit fixes a NullPointerException in the Quick Type Hierarchy by refactoring how the `visited` set is initialized and reset. It removes the `inputChanged` method and moves its logic into `internalExpandToLevel`, adding conditional `if` blocks and a new local variable (`reset`). This is a complex structural refactoring involving method deletion, code relocation, new variable declarations, and new control flow, which is beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities.

commitMessage:
Add syntax coloring for records

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2009
categoryReason: This commit adds new UI functionality (semantic highlighting) for a specific Java language feature.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support adding new classes (including nested/inner classes), adding new static fields, inserting elements into array initializers, and inserting new method calls as statements. This is currently not supported in the TriggerPattern DSL.
summary: This commit adds new semantic highlighting for Java `record` types in the JDT UI. This involves defining new NLS messages, adding a new static constant (`RECORD`), creating an entire new inner class (`RecordHighlighting`) with multiple overridden methods, and registering this new highlighter in an existing array. These are extensive structural changes and feature additions that are beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's pattern-matching and replacement capabilities.

commitMessage:
javadoc Markdown issues (#2051)

Partition based selection handling needs to handle markdown, too.

Fixes part of https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/1800
categoryReason: This category specifically addresses internal UI logic and behavior within Eclipse JDT, distinguishing it from broader Eclipse API modernization or general code style changes.
summary: This commit extends conditional logic in JDT's JavadocView to correctly handle Markdown comments. It modifies two boolean expressions: one to exclude Markdown partitions when checking for non-Javadoc content, and another to include Markdown partitions when identifying Javadoc-related regions. This ensures proper selection and content handling for Markdown-formatted Javadoc. These are direct 1:1 boolean expression replacements, fully implementable as two distinct GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules, improving internal JDT UI functionality for new content types.

commitMessage:
Fix completion of annotation in package-info.java to not fully qualify (#2050)

* Fix completion of annotation in package-info.java to not fully qualify

- fix LazyJavaTypeCompletionProposal.computeReplacementString() to
  not fully qualify if we are completing an annotation type which
  could already be computed to not be fully qualified (e.g. java.lang)
- add new test to CodeCompletionTest1d8
- fixes #2047

* Version bump(s) for 4.36 stream

---------

Co-authored-by: Eclipse JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: Fixes a bug in JDT's internal code completion logic related to annotation qualification.
languageChangeNeeded: The TriggerPattern DSL does not currently support bitwise/shift operators ('&', '|') in patterns or replacements, which are present in the new conditional expression. Additionally, directly replacing an entire 'if' statement, including its block, is not supported; the DSL operates at the expression or simple statement replacement level.
dslRuleAfterChange:
/* DSL extension would be needed to support bitwise operators. Example if supported:
org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.util.JavaModelUtil.isPackageInfo(fCompilationUnit)
:: fCompilationUnit != null
=> org.eclipse.jdt.internal.corext.util.JavaModelUtil.isPackageInfo(fCompilationUnit) && ((fProposal.getFlags() & 8192) == 0 || (fProposal.getFlags() & 512) == 0)
;; */
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's internal code completion logic for `package-info.java` by modifying a boolean condition within an `if` statement. The change adds logic to prevent fully qualifying annotation types that are already simple (e.g., from `java.lang`) unless they are not actual annotation types. The modified condition includes bitwise operators, which are not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL, making this transformation RED. Additionally, replacing an entire `if` statement block is beyond the DSL's current capabilities, which primarily focuses on expression and simple declaration replacements. The change is an internal bug fix, not a new Java language feature adoption, so the baseline Java 8 is sufficient for its context.

commitMessage:
Do not offer convert to static import when name collision exists (#2046)

* Do not offer convert to static import when name collision exists

- modify QuickAssistProcessor.getAddStaticImportProposals() to check
  for a collision with an unqualified reference
- add new UnqualifiedReferencesFinder class to QuickAssistProcessorUtil
- add new test to AssistQuickFixTest
- fixes #2045

* Version bump(s) for 4.36 stream

---------

Co-authored-by: Eclipse JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: Fixes a bug in JDT's internal quick assist logic related to static import proposals.
languageChangeNeeded: The TriggerPattern DSL does not support inserting new control flow statements like an 'if' statement block with a nested 'try-catch' block and new local variable declarations ('CompilationUnit cu = ...'). It is designed for pattern matching and replacement of existing expressions and declarations, not arbitrary code insertion.
summary: This commit addresses a bug in JDT's 'convert to static import' quick assist by adding a new validation block. It inserts an 'if' statement, containing a 'try-catch' block and a new local variable declaration (`CompilationUnit cu`), before existing logic to check for name collisions. If a collision is found, the quick assist is suppressed (`return false`). This is a complex structural code insertion involving new control flow, variable declarations, and `try-catch` blocks, which is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL. The `instanceof ICompilationUnit icu` syntax also indicates the use of Java 16 pattern matching for `instanceof`, reinforcing the complexity. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Change SuppressWarnings quick-fix to support multiple changes in file (#2039)

* Change SuppressWarnings quick-fix to support multiple changes in file

- replace SuppressWarningsProposalCore with SuppressWarningsFixCore
- modify SuppressWarningsBaseSubProcessor to support looking for
  adding all needed SuppressWarnings annotations in one change
- modify SuppressWarningsSubProcessor as needed
- add new SuppressWarningsFixCore and SuppressWarningsCleanUp
- add new test to QuickFixTest1d8
- fixes #1967

* Fix test suite failures due to proposal number changes
categoryReason: This commit represents a significant internal architectural refactoring of the JDT's SuppressWarnings quick-fix mechanism, rather than a direct code transformation pattern for user code.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support changing class inheritance, modifying constructor/method signatures, introducing new internal classes, or complex multi-step AST manipulation logic like refactoring quick-fix proposals to use a new core mechanism. It operates on pattern-matching and replacement of expressions or simple declarations, not architectural changes to the cleanup framework itself.
summary: This commit refactors the internal JDT UI SuppressWarnings quick-fix. It involves changing the class hierarchy of `SuppressWarningsProposal`, completely altering its constructor signature, removing and adding methods, and introducing a new internal core fix class (`SuppressWarningsFixCore`). These are deep structural and architectural changes to the cleanup infrastructure, not patterns that can be expressed or automated by the TriggerPattern DSL. The DSL's capabilities are limited to pattern-matching and replacing expressions or simple declaration structures, making this transformation RED for the current DSL.

commitMessage:
Fix reduce indentation to handle chain of if/else ifs/else (#2021)

* Add support to reduce indentation to support if/else if/ chain

- modify ReduceIndentationCleanUp.ReduceIndentationElseOperation to
  handle multiple ifs in an if/else if/ chain with possible multiple
  else ifs and an else
- modify processing of if statement to look down else ifs and
  add them to the operation list if applicable
- modify CleanUptest.testReduceIndentation() to add such a scenario
- fixes #1987

* Tweak fix to handle multiple embedded else-ifs
categoryReason: This commit is a bug fix for an existing JDT cleanup implementation (ReduceIndentationCleanUp), which involves complex changes to its internal logic for AST traversal and manipulation.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL is not designed for implementing complex algorithmic changes within a cleanup's logic, such as refactoring an AST visitor to recursively process if/else if/else chains, changing field types from single nodes to lists of nodes, or handling sophisticated AST manipulation via ListRewrite and conditional logic for different list sizes. These changes are deeply intertwined with the cleanup's internal implementation and control flow, which are beyond the expression-level replacement capabilities of the DSL.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the `ReduceIndentationCleanUp` by introducing a recursive helper method (`processIfStatement`), modifying existing boolean conditions for internal logic, changing a field from a single `IfStatement` to a `List<IfStatement>`, and extensively refactoring the `apply` method to handle complex `if/else if/else` chains with `ListRewrite` operations. These are significant structural and algorithmic changes to the *implementation* of an existing cleanup, involving complex control flow and AST manipulation logic. The TriggerPattern DSL is not capable of expressing such intricate, multi-step programmatic transformations, making this a RED classification.

commitMessage: disable custom folding preferences when not applicable (#2063)
categoryReason: The commit refactors internal UI preference block logic to manage master-slave relationships between controls, involving new fields, methods, and complex statement insertions. This is a common pattern in UI development that doesn't fit existing transformation categories focused on API migration or simple code style.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support structural changes like altering method signatures (return types), inserting local variable declarations from method call results, and adding arbitrary new statements (method invocations, `forEach` loops). Currently, it is limited to expression and annotation replacements, or specific declaration pattern matches.
summary: This commit refactors internal Eclipse JDT UI preference logic to implement master-slave dependencies between UI controls. It involves adding new fields (`fMasterSlaveListeners`), new methods (`addDependency`), modifying method signatures (changing `void` to `Button` or `Text`), and inserting new local variable assignments and method calls (`initiallyFoldCustomRegions = addCheckBox(...)`, `addDependency(...)`, `fMasterSlaveListeners.forEach(...)`). These changes are highly structural and involve adding new control flow and declarations, which are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL that focuses on expression, annotation, and specific declaration replacements.

commitMessage:
Add Filter Mechanism for Target in Move Refactoring - with caching (#2034)

* Add Filter Mechanism for Target in Move Refactoring #1337

- Text Search added to search withing the tree while moving (refactoring) the file to different package
- use caching to improve performance
- fixes #1337

---------

Co-authored-by: shahzaib.ibrahim <shahzaib.ibrahim@yatta.de>
categoryReason: The commit introduces a new UI feature (filtered tree) and internal caching logic within a refactoring component, which constitutes a refactoring of existing UI logic.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires DSL extensions for structural code generation (new methods, complex object initialization involving multiple statements, pattern matching instanceof variable declaration transformation, and multi-statement replacements for UI component setup).
summary: This commit introduces a new filter mechanism for move refactoring in JDT UI, involving several architectural and UI-specific changes. Key transformations include adopting Java 16+ pattern matching for `instanceof`, introducing a new private caching method using `computeIfAbsent` with a complex lambda, and replacing a direct `TreeViewer` instantiation with a `FilteredTree` setup. These changes are largely structural, introducing new methods, modifying control flow, and involving complex object instantiation patterns. The current TriggerPattern DSL is designed for expression-level and simple declaration-level rewrites, and cannot directly generate new methods, complex multi-statement lambda bodies, or transform `instanceof` checks into pattern variable declarations. Adding braces to single-statement `if` blocks is a stylistic change not typically handled by semantic transformation DSLs. Therefore, this commit is classified as RED, requiring significant DSL extensions to implement the described transformations.

commitMessage:
Fix too many folding regions in anonymous class

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2022
categoryReason: The commit introduces internal logic to refine code folding behavior within a JDT UI component.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support defining new helper methods within the hint file or injecting complex control flow statements (like `if` blocks with `return`) into existing code. It primarily handles expression-level or simple statement-level pattern replacements. To implement this, the DSL would need extensions for programmatic AST modification or full custom Java code execution.
summary: The commit addresses Bug 2022 by fixing too many folding regions in anonymous classes. It introduces a new private helper method `containsAnonymousChild` and uses it within a new `if` guard inside the `switch` statement for `IJavaElement.FIELD` and `IJavaElement.METHOD` types. This change involves adding a new method and inserting complex control flow, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for direct implementation. Thus, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
[24] Remove BETA_JAVA24 disclaimers - from Compiler preferences

https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2048
irrelevantReason: This commit primarily involves removing specific multi-line Javadoc disclaimers related to a BETA Java version and updating an internal method's logic to always return 'false' (effectively removing the beta version check). These changes are highly specific to the Eclipse JDT's internal release cycle management and documentation, rather than being generalizable, reusable code transformation patterns for user code or broadly applicable API modernizations. The removal of multi-line Javadoc comments is not a code transformation supported by the DSL, and the method logic change is too specific to the internal `isBetaVersion` method to be considered a reusable pattern.
summary: The commit removes specific Java 24 beta disclaimers from Javadoc and modifies an internal `isBetaVersion` method to always return `false`. This is an internal maintenance task related to product release cycles and not a generalizable code transformation pattern suitable for DSL rules. The changes are too specific to the internal JDT codebase to be broadly reusable.

commitMessage: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/BETA_JAVA24'
categoryReason: These rules improve the internal logic of Eclipse JDT UI components related to content type recognition, specifically for Javadoc and Markdown comments. This falls under refactoring internal Eclipse JDT UI features for correctness.
summary: This commit merges changes from the `BETA_JAVA24` branch, primarily updating internal JDT UI logic in `JavadocView.java` to recognize the `JAVA_MARKDOWN_COMMENT` partition. This involves extending two boolean conditions with an `&&` or `||` clause, adding a check for the new partition type. This ensures the Javadoc view's completion and element-at-offset logic correctly accounts for Markdown-formatted Javadoc, a feature that gained prominence with Java 18 (JEP 413). The change in `ComplianceConfigurationBlock.java` (modifying `@SuppressWarnings` on a method parameter and updating a comment) is not directly implementable by the DSL. The two identified boolean expression modifications are direct 1:1 replacements, making them fully implementable as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules. The rules are guarded by `sourceVersionGE(18)` to align with the context of Markdown in Javadoc.

The change in `ComplianceConfigurationBlock.java` (modifying `@SuppressWarnings` on a method parameter and updating a comment) is not directly implementable by the DSL. The two identified boolean expression modifications are direct 1:1 replacements, making them fully implementable as GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules. The rules are guarded by `sourceVersionGE(18)` to align with the context of Markdown in Javadoc.

commitMessage:
Add new cleanup to remove unnecessary suppress warnings annotations (#2115)

- modify CleanUpRequirements constructor to allow specifying
  COMPILER_PR_SUPPRESS_WARNINGS enabled if the setting of
  COMPILER_PB_UNUSED_WARNING_TOKEN is set to error
- modify current UnusedSuppressWarningsCleanUp to properly set
  compiler options and to demand a fresh AST
- modify UnusedSuppressWarningsFixCore to handle being used both
  for quick fixes and a cleanup
- add UnusedSuppressWarningsCleanUp to jdt.ui plugin.xml as last
  clean up and also make it visible via the UnnecessaryCodeTabPage
- add new test to CleanUpTest1d8
- fixes #2114
categoryReason: The cleanup specifically targets the removal of @SuppressWarnings annotations when they are no longer necessary, which is a distinct category from general annotation migration or style fixes.
languageChangeNeeded: The current DSL can match @SuppressWarnings annotations, but it lacks the capability to semantically determine if an annotation is 'unnecessary' (i.e., if it suppresses no active compiler warnings in the annotated code element). A DSL extension would require a new guard function that queries the JDT compiler for active warnings related to the matched annotation or the code element it annotates.
summary: The commit adds a new Eclipse JDT cleanup to remove unnecessary @SuppressWarnings annotations. This is a valuable code quality improvement. However, the core logic of determining if an annotation is 'unnecessary' (i.e., if it suppresses any active compiler warnings) requires deep semantic analysis, type binding information, and compiler integration that is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. The provided diff only shows the registration of this new cleanup and its UI elements, not the actual transformation logic. Therefore, it is classified as RED, indicating it cannot be implemented with the current DSL without significant extensions to its semantic analysis capabilities.

commitMessage: Fix line delimiter used by text block input assistants (#2113)
categoryReason: The change is an internal refactoring within JDT UI components to correctly handle text editor line delimiters.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently cannot introduce new local variable declarations (e.g., 'String delimiter = ...;') and then reference these local variables in subsequent replacement expressions. This commit requires either: 1. A DSL extension to allow local variable introduction before expression replacements. 2. Fully executable @FixFunction blocks that can perform AST manipulations to insert statements and modify references.
summary: This commit fixes a text block formatting bug in JDT UI input assistants by replacing `System.lineSeparator()` with a document-specific line delimiter obtained via `TextUtilities.getDefaultLineDelimiter(document)`. In one context (`AddTextBlockAction`), this involves introducing a new local variable for the delimiter and then using it in string concatenations. In another context (`JavaMultiLineStringAutoIndentStrategy`), the `document` is a method parameter, allowing `TextUtilities.getDefaultLineDelimiter(document)` to be directly used. However, the requirement to introduce a local variable (and another local variable it depends on) in `AddTextBlockAction` is beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities for direct pattern-based replacements. While the replacement logic is clear, the structural change of adding a new variable declaration statement prevents direct automation via the existing DSL. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Ensure forceReconciling is called after initialProcess if it was ignored

https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2074
categoryReason: The commit addresses a bug within JDT's internal JavaReconciler component.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need capabilities to:
1. Introduce new private helper methods (e.g., `initialProcessDone()`, `isInitialProcessDone()`).
2. Replace a single statement (e.g., `fIninitalProcessDone= true;`) with a multi-statement block that includes new control flow (`if`) and a lambda expression (`Job.createSystem(...)`) which schedules a new Job. This is a complex statement-level structural transformation that goes beyond simple expression replacement or annotation addition.

While some parts, like `!fIninitalProcessDone` changing to `!isInitialProcessDone()`, are simple expression replacements, they rely on the *creation* of the `isInitialProcessDone()` method, which the current DSL cannot do. The core fix involves new API introduction and complex control flow, not just pattern matching and simple rewriting.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's internal `JavaReconciler` by ensuring `forceReconciling()` is called if it was previously ignored due to ongoing `initialProcess()`. The fix introduces two new private synchronized helper methods (`initialProcessDone()` and `isInitialProcessDone()`) and modifies existing methods to use this new state management logic. Specifically, a boolean field access `fIninitalProcessDone` is replaced by a method call `isInitialProcessDone()`, and a simple field assignment `fIninitalProcessDone= true;` is replaced by an `if` block that conditionally schedules a `Job` using a lambda expression. This requires the introduction of new methods and complex structural changes involving control flow and lambda expressions, which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED.

commitMessage: Changed that single liner dont get folding button
categoryReason: This change specifically targets a UI behavior within JDT's internal text folding mechanism.
summary: This commit changes the logic for determining whether a code block should receive a folding button in the JDT editor. Specifically, it modifies a boolean condition `endLine > startLine` to `endLine > startLine + 1`. This means a folding button will only appear if a block spans at least two lines after the start line (i.e., at least three lines in total). This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement of a numerical literal within a boolean comparison. The pattern is highly specific to the internal JDT UI folding logic, so a class-specific guard is necessary. It is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule and addresses a UI feature enhancement/fix, making the code more precise for single-line code blocks.

commitMessage:
Completion within markdown should escape array brackets #3773 (#2112)

Get the new display string for completion proposal via new API CompletionProposal#getDisplayString()
categoryReason: This change updates how display strings are retrieved for code completion proposals within JDT's internal UI components.
summary: This commit modernizes internal JDT UI code completion logic by replacing calls to `methodProposal.getCompletion()` with `methodProposal.getDisplayString()`. This change ensures that the correct display string (which includes necessary escaping for contexts like markdown) is used for completion proposals. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement, applicable to instances of `org.eclipse.jdt.core.CompletionProposal`. The transformation is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule with an `instanceof` guard to ensure correct targeting. It improves the correctness and robustness of JDT's content assist feature.

commitMessage:
Remove dead code for problems never reported for Java < 1.8

See https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/pull/3883
and especially
https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/commit/af83290c268e95d01e3c8e6f571c4e909b9efbac
categoryReason: This commit involves internal code cleanup and API modernization within JDT's quick-fix processing infrastructure.
summary: This commit primarily removes dead code and old Java version-specific problem IDs from JDT's internal quick-fix processor (`QuickFixProcessor.java`, `LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor.java`, `ModifierCorrectionSubProcessor.java`, `TypeArgumentMismatchSubProcessor.java`). The most significant changes are structural (method deletions, removal of large blocks of `case` statements) which are not directly expressible as pattern-based DSL rules. However, one specific transformation involves renaming an internal `IProblem` constant from `IProblem.OuterLocalMustBeFinal` to `IProblem.OuterLocalMustBeEffectivelyFinal`. This is a direct 1:1 constant replacement, reflecting a Java 8 language concept ('effectively final'), and is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. While much of the commit is RED due to structural changes, this specific pattern is a clean, automatable refactoring for internal JDT API consistency.

commitMessage:
Fix ClassCastException when switching tabs

This commit resolves an exception that occurs when Control.getParent()
returns a CTabFolder instead of a TabItem. This fix ensures that the
Module Dependencies Tab view is displayed correctly.

Fixes : https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2146
categoryReason: This change specifically addresses a UI bug in Eclipse JDT, adapting to different SWT widget hierarchies.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support inserting new blocks of statements, including conditional (if) statements with embedded loops and their corresponding logic. Currently, the DSL is limited to expression and simple statement replacements, not arbitrary control flow insertion.
summary: The commit fixes a ClassCastException in the Module Dependencies Tab view by extending the parent widget handling logic. It adds a new 'if' block to check if the parent is a 'CTabFolder' (using Java 14+ pattern matching for instanceof) and applies corresponding logic. This involves structural changes—adding a new conditional block with a loop—which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based replacements.

commitMessage:
Make the new folding default (#2150)

- make new extended folding option default
- change preferences messages so the option is no longer marked as experimental
categoryReason: Modifies an Eclipse preference setting, changing its default value.
summary: This commit changes the default value of the Eclipse JDT UI preference `EDITOR_NEW_FOLDING_ENABLED` from `false` to `true`. This is a direct 1:1 replacement of a boolean literal within a method call, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. Changes in the `.properties` file are not applicable for DSL transformation.

commitMessage:
[#2157] Describe final field initialization proposal considering type (#2158)

- InitializeFinalFieldProposal now selects more appropriate description
using updateType passed to it, which helps better distinguish both types
of corrections in UI.

Signed-off-by: Nikifor Fedorov <nikifor.fedorov@arsysop.ru>
categoryReason: Refactors internal JDT UI logic related to correction proposals, specifically how descriptive messages are generated.
summary: This commit modifies the first argument passed to the `super` constructor in `InitializeFinalFieldProposal`. The argument, a string formatted via `Messages.format()`, is replaced with a more complex ternary expression that dynamically selects a description based on `updateType`. This is a direct expression replacement, including nested method calls and a ternary operator, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL for internal JDT UI logic refactoring.

commitMessage: extract to local variable - minor fix
categoryReason: Applies an 'Extract Local Variable' refactoring within internal JDT UI code, which involves multi-statement insertion and replacement.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for 'Extract Local Variable' refactoring pattern, including: 1) identifying common subexpressions, 2) inserting a new local variable declaration before usage, 3) replacing all occurrences of the subexpression with the new variable, and 4) restructuring control flow from `if-if` to `if-else if`.
summary: This commit performs an 'Extract Local Variable' refactoring in `BuildPathBasePage.java`. It extracts `fSWTControl.getParent()` into a new local variable `parent` and updates subsequent `instanceof` checks to use this variable. Additionally, it restructures the two `if` statements into an `if-else if` block. This kind of multi-statement transformation, involving the insertion of a new declaration and multiple replacements, along with control flow restructuring, is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Synchronize ICompulationUnit stamp with underlined IResource after save
actions

JDT Java search has a special handling for modified working copies (see
BasicSearchEngine.getWorkingCopies()).

This fix synchronizes the modification stamps between the
CompilationUnitElementInfo and underlined IResource after "save actions"
execution triggered in
CompilationUnitDocumentProvider.commitWorkingCopy(), so the saved
working copy has correct state and Java search can find the type from
the index.

See https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/pull/3919 for the
first part of the change on JDT Core side.

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/3918
categoryReason: The change fixes an internal bug within the Eclipse JDT UI component related to resource synchronization, not a general user-facing code pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support inserting new control flow statements (like `if`/`else` blocks) or sequences of statements at a specific point in the AST (e.g., after a certain statement), rather than just replacing a matched pattern with an expression or a new sequence of expressions.
summary: The commit fixes a synchronization issue in `CompilationUnitDocumentProvider` by inserting an `if` statement to update the compilation unit's timestamp if it differs from the resource's stamp. This is a structural change involving the addition of new control flow (an `if` block with a statement), which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for direct code transformation.

commitMessage:
Fix `Create new member record preview`: formatting is off (#2184) Two superflous newlines (`
`) where causing the bold font styling to be place on the wrong chars. fixes #2183
categoryReason: The commit fixes a specific UI rendering bug within an internal JDT UI component, not a general code improvement pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for inserting new local variable declarations, wrapping existing statements in conditional blocks (if/else), and adding new cases to switch statements. These are structural AST transformations beyond expression replacement.
summary: The commit fixes a UI formatting bug in the 'Create new member record preview' by conditionally appending newline HTML tags. This involves adding a new local boolean variable (`noSpacingNeeded`), wrapping existing `StringBuilder.append()` calls within an `if` block, and adding a new `default` case to a `switch` statement to set the boolean flag. These are structural changes involving local variable declarations, conditional control flow, and switch statement modifications, which are not directly expressible as pattern-based replacements in the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Replace usages of new Image(device, width, height) (#2116)

Replacing it with imageGcDrawer
categoryReason: The change involves a significant structural transformation: extracting existing, potentially multi-line drawing logic (including GC setup and variable captures) into a newly created `ImageGcDrawer` functional interface implementation. This is beyond the current DSL's capabilities for expression replacement or simple lambda conversion.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs a mechanism to: 1. Identify a block of statements (GC initialization, drawing commands) that should be encapsulated. 2. Extract this block into a newly constructed lambda or anonymous class body for a functional interface like `ImageGcDrawer`. 3. Handle the capture of external variables (e.g., `fLTR`) by the generated lambda/anonymous class. 4. Potentially infer or automatically generate the `getGcStyle()` method implementation in the anonymous class, if present.
summary: This commit replaces `new Image(Device, width, height)` with `new Image(Device, ImageGcDrawer, width, height)`. This is an SWT API modernization to use functional image drawing, especially for high-DPI displays. While one instance uses a simple empty lambda, other instances require extracting several lines of GC setup and drawing logic (including capturing local variables and interacting with the `GC` object) into an `ImageGcDrawer` anonymous class or lambda, including its `getGcStyle()` method. The current DSL is not capable of performing such complex structural code generation (moving multiple statements into a new lambda body). Therefore, this is classified as a RED transformation.

commitMessage:
Remove obsolete specification of hover images (#2189)

Specifying hover images that are the same as the usual enabled-version
images is unnecessary and the usage of different hover images has been
obsolete for quite some time in Eclipse platform.

This change removes remaining specifications of hover images.
categoryReason: The change removes usage of an obsolete API method (`setHoverImageDescriptor`), which is a direct statement deletion. This simplifies UI code by removing redundant calls.
summary: This commit removes redundant calls to `setHoverImageDescriptor()` from various UI actions and an internal image utility. These calls are obsolete because the hover image is now typically the same as the enabled image, or the concept of separate hover images is no longer relevant. This is a straightforward code cleanup, directly implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule by matching the method call and replacing it with an empty string (implying deletion). The rule applies to any instance of `setHoverImageDescriptor()` on a generic `$action` object. It improves code readability and removes unnecessary API calls, aligning with Eclipse API modernization. No specific Java version guard is needed as this is an internal Eclipse API cleanup that applies from the Java 8 baseline.
